title: terraform apply panics with nil pointer dereference in azurerm provider 3.45.0
body: Steps to reproduce: provision a resource_group with a kv block. On apply, Terraform exits with a nil pointer in the azurerm provider plugin. Stack trace attached.
label: bug

title: S3 bucket destroy hangs indefinitely with versioning enabled
body: Calling terraform destroy on an aws_s3_bucket with versioning=true and ~12k versioned objects hangs forever. The provider logs show polling for completion but no progress after 90 minutes.
label: bug

title: Regression: terraform init no longer recognizes the lock file after upgrading 1.5 to 1.6
body: After upgrading to 1.6.4, init refuses to start, claiming the provider hash in .terraform.lock.hcl is invalid. Reverting to 1.5 makes the same project work again.
label: bug

title: terraform validate reports false positive errors on partial backend configuration
body: With an s3 backend that uses partial config (only bucket and key set, the rest via -backend-config), validate fails on what looks like a complete config from the runtime's POV.
label: bug

title: Workspace deletion silently fails when remote state holds a lock
body: Deleting a workspace via the CLI exits 0 even when the state is locked by another in-flight apply. The workspace then ends up in a half-deleted state visible only via the API.
label: bug

title: Plan output is non-deterministic for sets of resources with the same tags
body: On repeated `terraform plan` runs against the same config, the order of items inside a tag set varies, producing churn in the human-readable plan output even when no real change exists.
label: bug

title: Add support for assume_role_with_web_identity to the aws provider
body: It would be useful to authenticate the AWS provider using OIDC tokens (e.g. from GitHub Actions) without needing static credentials. Could the provider accept web_identity_token + web_identity_token_file?
label: feature

title: Allow setting custom HTTP headers in the http data source
body: Some internal APIs require an Authorization header. Right now the http data source only supports request_headers via env. A first-class `request_headers` argument would unblock several uses.
label: feature

title: Request: native JSON output for terraform fmt -check
body: Currently fmt -check exits non-zero on diffs but emits human-readable output. A -json flag matching `terraform plan -json` would let CI tools render the diff structurally.
label: feature

title: Support for IAM Identity Center (formerly AWS SSO) profiles in the aws provider
body: The aws provider supports static credentials and assume_role, but not directly reading from an IAM Identity Center profile in ~/.aws/config. Could this be added natively?
label: feature

title: Native OpenTelemetry traces from terraform apply
body: Would be valuable to emit OTLP traces during apply so we can see provider RPC latency in our existing observability stack rather than parsing -json logs.
label: feature

title: Allow ignore_changes on a computed nested attribute
body: Today ignore_changes works at the top level but not on nested attributes inside a dynamic block. A path expression syntax (e.g. ignore_changes = [tags['Owner']]) would help.
label: feature

title: Typo in resource provisioner remote-exec documentation
body: The example in remote-exec docs has 'inine' where it should be 'inline'. See https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/provisioners/remote-exec
label: docs

title: Docs missing example for for_each on modules
body: The for_each meta-argument on modules was added in 0.13 but the Registry documentation still only mentions count. Could you add a worked example?
label: docs

title: Upgrade guide skips the 0.13upgrade step
body: The 0.12-to-0.13 upgrade page documents the state-format migration but doesn't mention that you must first run `terraform 0.13upgrade`. Several users have been bitten by this.
label: docs

title: Clarify what happens to in-flight applies when a workspace is deleted
body: The docs say workspace deletion is permitted, but don't say whether an in-flight apply continues, is cancelled, or leaves orphaned resources.
label: docs

title: Example needed: sensitive=true on output blocks
body: The docs mention output sensitivity exists, but there is no end-to-end example showing how sensitive outputs propagate (or don't) into a parent module's plan output.
label: docs

title: aws_eks_cluster docs don't list the required IAM policies
body: The docs walk through creating the cluster but never enumerate the IAM policies the calling principal needs. Several users hit AccessDenied with no obvious next step.
label: docs

title: How do I migrate state from local backend to S3 backend safely?
body: I have ~300 resources in a terraform.tfstate file. What's the recommended migration path to an S3 backend without downtime or duplicate resource creation? Should I use terraform state mv?
label: question

title: What's the best pattern for managing 100+ AWS accounts?
body: We're at ~25 accounts and growing. Is the official recommendation one workspace per account, one repo per account, or a single config with assume_role per account?
label: question

title: Can I use count and for_each on the same resource?
body: The docs say count and for_each are mutually exclusive on a single resource, but I see examples online that appear to mix them. Which is correct, and is this changing?
label: question

title: Recommended way to share state between workspaces without terraform_remote_state
body: Our security team flagged terraform_remote_state because it grants broad read access. Is there an officially blessed alternative for exposing a small set of values from one workspace to another?
label: question

title: What's the best way to handle secrets in Terraform without committing them?
body: We use Vault for runtime secrets but have a few that bootstrap Terraform itself (provider credentials). What does HashiCorp recommend for this chicken-and-egg case?
label: question

title: How do I import an existing AWS resource using only its ARN?
body: terraform import requires the resource-specific ID format (e.g. instance-id for EC2). Is there a way to import by ARN directly, since that's the only identifier we have for some legacy resources?
label: question

title: Should I use one config or one workspace per environment for staging/prod?
body: We currently have a single repo with -var-file=staging.tfvars / prod.tfvars. Friend says workspaces are deprecated for this; another says they're fine. What is the current best practice?
label: question
